---
title: "ChatGatewayElement"
description: "Production boundary for submitting messages, streaming tokens, and cancelling turns from application code"
lightbox: true
---

The `ChatGatewayElement` is a transport-agnostic boundary for driving Pyllments chat flows from backend or service code. Unlike `ChatInterfaceElement` (Panel UI) or `APIElement` (blocking HTTP futures), it exposes a Python API for:

- **Send** — inject a user `MessagePayload` into the graph with one call
- **Receive** — stream assistant tokens/events through a `TurnHandle`
- **Cancel** — stop in-flight generation and close the provider stream

Use it when your application owns the HTTP/WebSocket layer (FastAPI, SSE, custom stream broker) and Pyllments owns the LLM graph.

## When to use this element

| Element | Best for |
|---------|----------|
| `ChatGatewayElement` | Production apps: programmatic submit/stream/cancel |
| `ChatInterfaceElement` | Browser GUI with Panel |
| `APIElement` | Simple request/response HTTP endpoints (atomic responses) |
| `PipeElement` | Debugging and testing flows |

## Instantiation

**Arguments:**

`default_aggregate_stream: bool = True`<br>
<span class="tab">When assistant messages arrive in `stream` mode, whether `MessageModel.aggregate_stream` is enabled so `content` and `tool_calls` are updated during consumption. Keep `True` when `HistoryHandlerElement` (or similar) must await a completed message. Set `False` only if your app fully owns aggregation and history is handled separately.</span>

`pending_db_path: str, optional`<br>
<span class="tab">SQLite path for pending tool permission requests. Use this when pending approvals should survive app refresh or backend restart.</span>

## Application API

### `submit_message(content, role='user', **kwargs) -> TurnHandle`

Creates a user `MessagePayload`, registers the turn, and schedules emission on `message_output`. Returns immediately with a `TurnHandle`.

Additional `**kwargs` are passed through to `MessagePayload` / `MessageModel` (for example `mode`, `timestamp`).

### `submit_message_async(content, role='user', **kwargs) -> TurnHandle`

Same as `submit_message`, but awaits `message_output` emission. Prefer this in async tests or when you need the message on the wire before continuing.

### `cancel(turn_id: str) -> None`

Cancels a turn by id. If an assistant message is already linked, calls `MessageModel.cancel()` to tear down the provider stream.

### `TurnHandle`

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `stream()` | Async iterator of `MessageStreamEvent` (tokens, tool deltas, completion). Waits until the assistant `MessagePayload` is linked to this turn. |
| `final_message()` | Awaits the completed assistant `MessagePayload`. Consumes the stream if not already read. |
| `cancel()` | Delegates to `gateway.cancel(turn_id)`. |

**Example:**

```python
from pyllments.elements import ChatGatewayElement, LLMChatElement, HistoryHandlerElement

gateway = ChatGatewayElement()
llm = LLMChatElement(output_mode='stream', generate_content_on_emit=False)
history = HistoryHandlerElement(history_token_limit=10000)

gateway.ports.message_output > history.ports.payload_emit_input
history.ports.context_output > llm.ports.messages_emit_input
llm.ports.message_output > gateway.ports.assistant_message_input
llm.ports.message_output > history.ports.payload_input

turn = gateway.submit_message("Hello")

async for event in turn.stream():
    if event.type == "token":
        broker.publish(event.content_delta)

assistant = await turn.final_message()
```

::: {.callout-important}
Call `turn.stream()` or `await turn.final_message()` for each turn. Assistant streams are consumed once; `HistoryHandlerElement` waiting on `await_ready()` will not complete until the stream finishes or is cancelled.
:::

::: {.callout-note}
Configure `LLMChatElement` with `generate_content_on_emit=False` and `output_mode='stream'` so the gateway (or your app) consumes the provider stream instead of the LLM element draining it before emit.
:::

## Turn matching

Phase 1 uses **FIFO** matching: the oldest pending turn receives the next assistant `MessagePayload` on `assistant_message_input`.

Cancelled turns discard or cancel late-arriving assistant messages.

## Input Ports

| Port Name | Payload Type | Behavior |
|-----------|--------------|----------|
| `assistant_message_input` | `MessagePayload` | Receives assistant responses (typically from `LLMChatElement.message_output`). Links payload to the pending turn and applies `default_aggregate_stream` for stream mode. |
| `tool_use_input` | `ToolUsePayload` | Receives tool-use payloads from `ToolUseElement.tool_use_output`. Invokes application hooks, auto-executes approved/no-permission records, and registers permission requests when needed. |

: {.hover}

## Output Ports

| Port Name | Payload Type | Behavior |
|-----------|--------------|----------|
| `message_output` | `MessagePayload` | Emits user messages into the flow (history, context builder, etc.). |
| `tool_events_output` | `StructuredPayload` | Emits `{"tool_calls": list}` when a stream completes with tool calls. |
| `tool_result_output` | `ToolUsePayload` | Emits executed or denied tool-use payloads for history/UI/context. |

: {.hover}

## Tool-Use Permission and Execution Flow

The gateway owns permission timing and execution of approved tool records. Tool
implementations remain in ``ToolUseElement``; hydrated payloads rebind automatically
through ``ToolUsePayload``'s name-based executor registry.

```python
tool_use_el = ToolUseElement(name="main_tools", functions=[...])
gateway = ChatGatewayElement()

tool_use_el.ports.tool_use_output > gateway.ports.tool_use_input
gateway.ports.tool_result_output > history_handler_el.ports.payload_emit_input
```

When a `ToolUsePayload` arrives on `tool_use_input`:

- `on_tool_use(notice)` receives a `ToolUseNotice` containing handles for every tool call in the payload, whether or not permission is required. Use this for app-visible auditing, progress UI, or conceal/show decisions.
- Records that do not require permission are auto-executed after the notice hook returns.
- Records that require permission are persisted (optional pending store) and surfaced via `on_permission_request(request)`.
- Executed or denied results are emitted on `tool_result_output`.

When a `ToolUsePayload` contains records where `permission_required=True` and `status='awaiting_permission'`, the gateway builds a `ToolPermissionRequest`. Its `tools` list contains only the tool calls awaiting approval. Your application can decide immediately:

```python
async def on_permission_request(request):
    for tool in request.tools:
        app.show_tool_prompt(
            name=tool.name,
            description=tool.description,
            parameters=tool.parameters,
        )

    if await app.user_approved():
        request.approve_all()
    else:
        request.deny_all(reason="User declined")
```

If the UI resolves later, keep the request object and pass it back after mutating tool decisions:

```python
pending_request = request
pending_request.tools[0].approve()
await gateway.resolve_permission_request(pending_request)
```

When `on_permission_request` returns with decisions already set, the gateway resolves the request automatically. `resolve_permission_request_sync(request)` schedules the same resolution on the runtime loop for synchronous UI callbacks.

For cold start, configure `pending_db_path` or pass a `pending_store`, recreate the `ToolUseElement` instances with the same `name` values stored on pending payloads, then call `await gateway.hydrate_pending_tool_uses()` to restore pending prompts via `on_pending_permission_restored(request)`.

Name important `ToolUseElement` instances explicitly (for example `name="main_tools"` or `name=f"{chat_id}:main_tools"`) so serialized payloads can rebind after restart.

## Tool events

While iterating `turn.stream()`, when a `tool_calls_complete` event is observed, the gateway emits on `tool_events_output`:

```python
{"tool_calls": [{"id": "...", "type": "function", "function": {...}}]}
```

Wire `tool_events_output` to `ToolUseElement.tool_request_message_input` (via a message builder) or handle tool routing in application code.

## Related documentation

- [ToolUseElement](../ToolUseElement/index.qmd) — tool orchestration and execution
- [MessagePayload](../../explanation/MessagePayload.qmd) — streaming, `MessageStreamEvent`, and cancellation on the model
- [LLMChatElement](../LLMChatElement/index.qmd) — LLM backend and `generate_content_on_emit`
- [Gateway Chat recipe](../../recipes/gateway_chat/index.qmd) — reference wiring
